title: Ustekinumab (Stelara) — UHC Medical Policy (Moderate-to-Severe Crohn's Disease)
sourceText: Coverage criteria for ustekinumab (Stelara) in moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease. Covers IV induction (J3358) and SC maintenance (J3357) per FDA-approved indication.

criteria.text: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease confirmed by endoscopy and/or imaging with histopathology consistent with Crohn's disease
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for documented diagnosis of Crohn's disease with ICD-10 K50.x codes, plus a colonoscopy/ileoscopy report describing characteristic findings (skip lesions, aphthous or linear ulcers, terminal ileum involvement) and/or pathology confirming non-caseating granulomas or chronic active inflammation consistent with CD.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload the colonoscopy report and pathology report establishing the diagnosis of Crohn's disease.

criteria.text: Documented inadequate response, loss of response, or intolerance to at least one preferred conventional therapy (corticosteroids, immunomodulators) AND at least one TNF-alpha inhibitor (adalimumab, infliximab, certolizumab) — step therapy requirement
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for explicit documentation of trials of named agents with start/end dates, doses, and outcome (failure / loss of response / intolerance / contraindication). Both steps must be satisfied: (a) conventional therapy and (b) TNF inhibitor.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload a medication and treatment history document listing each prior agent, the dose and duration, and the documented reason for discontinuation (failure, loss of response, intolerance, or contraindication).

criteria.text: Active disease evidenced by clinical symptoms (e.g. abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss) and/or objective markers (elevated CRP, fecal calprotectin, or active findings on imaging/endoscopy) at the time of request
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for current symptoms documented in a recent (within 90 days) progress note, plus any objective evidence of active inflammation: elevated CRP/ESR, fecal calprotectin, or imaging/scope findings showing ongoing disease activity.
criteria.uploadHint: Upload a recent GI progress note or lab report documenting active disease (symptoms + objective inflammatory markers).

criteria.text: Prescribing provider is a gastroenterologist or in consultation with a gastroenterologist
criteria.evidenceHint: Look for the ordering or attesting physician's specialty in the request form, letter of medical necessity, or progress note header. Specialty 'Gastroenterology' explicitly stated, or co-signature from a gastroenterologist.
